Spawned a throwaway task E-1173 from inside the worktree:

```
PYTHONPATH=src endless task spawn E-1173 --worktree /tmp/e-1144-spawn-test --no-plan
```

Verified:
- New window appeared with name `endless_test-spawn[E-1173]`.
- `tmux set -w -t 'endless_test-spawn[E-1173]' @endless_task_id 1173` — exit 0.
- `tmux show -wv -t 'endless_test-spawn[E-1173]' @endless_task_id` returned `1173`.
- Cleaned up window + throwaway task + tmp files.

## Follow-up tasks filed

- **E-1173** (ready, phase=next): "Record Claude session UUIDs per tmux pane to enable crash recovery." Motivated by an unrelated tmux crash that occurred during this session, which exposed that nothing in Endless tracks the (tmux-pane → claude-session-UUID) mapping needed to resume sessions after a crash. Plan file at `~/.init/.endless/plans/E-1173.md`.

fields.outcome: Shipped on main as merge 14d1cb6 (commit a25aa58). Final format <project>_<slug>[E-nnn] (e.g. 'endless_tmux-label[E-1144]'). Separator is '_' — the spawn prompt's proposed ':' breaks tmux -t targeting (session:window), and Mike's first alternative '.' also breaks it (window.pane); '_' was the third choice. Slug uses a 14-word stop-list and takes the first 1-2 remaining title tokens. Plan/decisions in --text. Follow-up E-1173 filed (Claude-session UUID tracking, motivated by an out-of-scope tmux crash during this session).

payload.title: Define task lifecycle metadata requirements for description, outcome, and plan
payload.description: Decide what task fields are required when, who owns each, and what should soft-warn vs hard-block. Drives the implementation tasks for --draft, soft warnings, and 'task prompt' validation.

payload.title: Require --description on task add by default; add --draft flag to bypass for human filers
payload.description: Make 'endless task add' require --description for non-human callers (Claude/agents). Humans pass --draft to defer authoring; Claude can fill it in later. Forces delegator sessions to populate description at file-time so the spawned session inherits a clean contract.

payload.title: Add soft warnings at verify transition when description or outcome is empty
payload.description: On 'endless task update --status verify' (and the assumed shortcut), print a stderr warning if description or outcome is empty. Do NOT block — completed work matters more than perfect metadata. No warning for missing plan/text since most tasks legitimately don't need one.

payload.title: Block 'endless task prompt' and 'task spawn' when description is missing
payload.description: Block 'endless task prompt <id>' and 'endless task spawn <id>' if the task has no description. Error message tells the caller to add one first. Catches the case where a delegator session bypassed the file-time check (e.g. with --draft) and then tries to spawn — the spawned session would have no clean contract to inherit.

fields.text:
# E-1175 — Task lifecycle metadata: who owns description / outcome / plan, and when

## Context

`endless task spawn` was originally introduced for one use case: an ad-hoc Claude session writes a prompt for an unrelated NEW session, hands it off, and walks away. Single shot, low ceremony.

It was later adopted for a second use case: a long-running **delegator** session running in `now-ready-auto` mode that picks tasks off the ready queue and spawns implementation sessions for each. Higher cadence, multiple parallel sessions, requires a clean per-task contract.

The same `task add` + `task spawn` primitives serve both use cases today. The metadata bar that's adequate for the first is inadequate for the second, and we have no enforcement.

## What surfaced this

Session E-1144 (rename tmux window labels) was spawned by a delegator. When it finished:

- `description` field was empty (delegator didn't set one; spawned session didn't backfill).
- `text` (plan) was empty (forgivable — small task).
- `outcome` was empty (the most useful artifact: the prompt assumed `:` would work as a separator; reality required falling through `:` → `.` → `_` due to tmux's target-spec parsing rules; that journey is gone now unless someone reads the chat transcript).

A reader scanning `endless task list` saw only the title. The lessons from the session — non-obvious tmux delimiter behavior, the slug heuristic decision — were not captured.

## Decision

| Field         | Required when                          | Owner                                     | Enforcement                                                          |
|---------------|----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `description` | At `task add` for non-human callers     | Whoever files the task (delegator / Claude) | Hard: `task add` errors without it. Humans pass `--draft` to bypass. |
| `description` | Before `task prompt` / `task spawn`     | Whoever spawns                            | Hard: `task prompt` and `task spawn` refuse if missing.              |
| `outcome`     | Before `task update --status verify`    | Spawned session                           | Soft: warn to stderr, do not block.                                  |
| `plan` (text) | Optional always                        | Whoever has design content                | None. Most tasks legitimately have no plan.                          |

`description` is for the human/agent scanning a list — a 1-sentence "what is this." `prompt` is procedural instructions for a spawned session and does not substitute. `outcome` is the post-mortem written by whoever did the work — what shipped, what diverged from the prompt, what surprises were filed as follow-ups.

The hard gate at file-time forces delegator (Claude) callers to author description; the `--draft` flag preserves friction-free human filing where Claude will fill it in later. The hard gate at spawn-time catches the bypass case — a `--draft`-filed task with no description should not be spawnable until someone (probably Claude, on the human's behalf) authors the description.

## Why soft (not hard) on outcome

A completed change with no `outcome` field is still better than no change. Hard-gating the `verify` transition would punish the spawned session for finishing — exactly when we want it to ship. The warning is enough nudge for the right callers and ignorable for the wrong ones; the long-run answer is making outcome easy enough to write that nobody bothers skipping.

## Why no enforcement on plan

Most tasks don't need one. A "rename a flag" task with a 2-line title is self-documenting; a "design new IPC layer" task obviously needs a plan. Forcing a plan field when none is warranted devalues the field.

## Implementation

Three sibling tasks, all related to this decision and to E-1000 (which renames `text` → `plan` and is a prerequisite for using the term "plan" consistently):

- **E-1176** — `task add` requires `--description`; add `--draft` flag for humans.
- **E-1177** — Soft stderr warnings on `task update --status verify` when description or outcome empty.
- **E-1178** — `task prompt` and `task spawn` refuse when description is missing.

Order: E-1176 first (creates the field-required guarantee). E-1177 and E-1178 are independent of each other; either can land before the other.

## Behaviors expected of delegator sessions going forward (before code ships)

The CLI gates land later. In the meantime, delegator sessions should:

1. Always pass `--description` to `task add` when filing a task they intend to spawn.
2. Treat the `prompt` as procedural-only — do not duplicate the "what is this" content from the description into the prompt.
3. End each spawned session's instruction set with: "Before flipping status to verify, run `endless task update <id> --outcome <one-paragraph summary>`."

## Out of scope

- Auto-generating description from prompt's first sentence. Too magical; humans/Claude can do it deliberately.
- Forcing description on transitions other than `verify` and on commands other than `prompt` / `spawn`.
- Changing the spawn-prompt template content itself (separate concern; concerns the delegator's prompt-generation code).

## Related

- **E-1000** — Rename `tasks.text` column and CLI `--text` flag to `--plan`. Prerequisite for consistent terminology in this decision.
- **E-999** — Design field separation for plan content vs analysis. Adjacent / future.
- **E-1144** — Triggering example. The session whose missing metadata surfaced the gap.

# E-1130 — Skip endless-hook work when cwd is in a worktree but binary is the global one

## Context

E-998 ships a per-worktree `.claude/settings.json` so a Claude session inside a worktree fires `<worktree>/bin/endless-hook` instead of swapping the global `/usr/local/bin/endless-hook` symlink. E-1129 verified that Claude Code MERGES hook entries across user/project/local settings layers — does not replace. As a result, a session whose cwd is inside the worktree fires BOTH binaries for every event. State-mutating event handlers (`InitSession`, `ParseTranscript`, `SetProcess`, `RegisterTaskFile`, `EndSession`, …) execute twice for that session: duplicate DB rows, duplicate companion-file writes, duplicate task-context injection.

E-998's cross-session safety goal is intact (sessions outside the worktree still fire only the global), so this is a worktree-session-only side effect. The fix lives entirely in the global binary: when invoked from a cwd inside an endless-managed worktree that has its own `bin/endless-hook` (different file from the running binary), the global short-circuits before any state mutation. The worktree binary handles the event alone.

This is a developer-only concern — end users of endless never run two binaries on the same hook event.

## Approach

Add an early self-skip check in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` `runClaude`, immediately after `projectID` lookup (line 84) and BEFORE activity throttle/recording (line 87). All mutations — including `RecordActivity` — are guarded by the same skip.

### Skip logic (pseudocode)

```
if projectID == 0 || cwd == "" → no skip (run normally)
projectRoot ← monitor.ProjectPath(projectID)        // existing helper
worktreeRoot ← monitor.FindWorktreeRoot(cwd, projectRoot)  // existing helper
if worktreeRoot == "" → no skip (cwd is in main, foreign, or unrelated)

worktreeBin ← <worktreeRoot>/bin/endless-hook
worktreeStat ← os.Stat(worktreeBin)

if worktreeStat == nil and err is ErrNotExist:
    LOUD WARN: "cwd <cwd> is in worktree <worktreeRoot> but <worktreeBin> does not exist — running global as fallback. Run 'just build' in the worktree to enable the override."
    no skip (run normally)

if worktreeStat == nil and err is other:
    log error, no skip (run normally — never break the hook)

selfPath ← os.Executable()
selfStat ← os.Stat(selfPath)
if os.SameFile(selfStat, worktreeStat):
    no skip (I AM the worktree binary)

# We are the global, the worktree binary exists, we are inside the worktree:
log "deferring to <worktreeBin> (cwd is in worktree)"
return nil  // skip all event handling
```

### Why these choices

- **Identity check via `os.SameFile`.** Wraps the `(dev, ino)` comparison the user picked. Robust against the `/usr/local/bin/endless-hook` → `<main>/bin/endless-hook` symlink and any future symlink layout — `os.Stat` follows symlinks by default. No string/path matching.
- **Asymmetric design (only the global skips).** The worktree binary doesn't need to check anything — the global yielding is sufficient. This avoids a chicken-and-egg "should I run if the other is going to?" coordination.
- **Loud warn on missing worktree binary.** Per the configuration-must-loudly-complain principle: the hook still runs the global so the Claude session keeps working, but writes a clearly-marked message to both stderr and `<ConfigDir>/log/hook.log` (existing logger at `claude.go:23-40`). The user cannot miss a missed `just build`.
- **Skip BEFORE activity throttle.** The throttle would naturally dedup most concurrent fires, but not all (race within the throttle window) and the throttle has nothing to do with the architectural reason both fire. Skipping earlier is cleaner.
- **`os.Executable()` failure → no skip.** Defensive: if we can't determine our own path we run normally rather than break the hook.

## Critical files

- **`/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`** — add skip helper, call it from `runClaude` between current line 84 and line 86. Also add an import for any new stdlib (none new — `os`, `filepath`, `log` already imported).
- **`/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go`** — read-only: reuse `FindWorktreeRoot` (lines 220-245) verbatim.
- **`/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/internal/monitor/`** — read-only: reuse `ProjectPath` (already used by `handleWorktreeAdoption` at `claude.go:988`).

No new files. No package boundary changes. Skip helper lives as a private function in `claude.go` next to `handleWorktreeAdoption` (logically related).

## Tests

Two layers:

**Unit (Go).** New `cmd/endless-hook/claude_skip_test.go`. Cases:
- cwd outside any project → no skip
- cwd in main checkout (no worktree companion above it) → no skip
- cwd in worktree, worktree binary missing → no skip + warn log captured (assert via redirected log output)
- cwd in worktree, worktree binary exists, self == worktree binary → no skip
- cwd in worktree, worktree binary exists, self != worktree binary → SKIP

For the file-identity cases, the test creates a temp directory with `<wt>/.endless/worktree.json` (companion sentinel) and a `<wt>/bin/endless-hook` file. The "self" is faked by passing in the path used as `os.Executable` via a small seam (an injected function or a package-level var the test overrides). The exploration's logger writes to stderr+file via `log.SetOutput`; the test redirects `log.SetOutput` to a `bytes.Buffer` to assert the warn message.

**End-to-end (manual, recorded in commit).** From inside `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN/`:
1. `just build` → ensures `bin/endless-hook` exists.
2. `just claude-settings-init` → installs `.claude/settings.json`.
3. `endless task spawn <task>` → opens a tmux pane with Claude.
4. In that Claude session, type any prompt (fires UserPromptSubmit).
5. Tail `<ConfigDir>/log/hook.log` and grep for the new "deferring to" log line — should see exactly one (the global's deferral). The worktree binary's normal hook handling lines should also appear.
6. Query the events DB for rows with that session_id at the prompt timestamp — should be exactly one set, not two.
7. Spawn a second Claude session OUTSIDE any worktree (e.g., from `~/`). Confirm no "deferring to" line — global runs normally because cwd is not in a worktree.

## Out of scope

- Changing `claude-settings-init` to write `settings.local.json` instead — same merge rule applies, no benefit.
- Rolling back E-998 — sibling-session safety is the bigger win and stays.
- Detecting a "stale" worktree settings.json (settings file present but worktree gone) — the recipe is paired with worktree creation; mismatch only happens if someone hand-deletes `bin/` after running the recipe. The loud-warn covers it.
- Any change to the worktree binary's behavior — asymmetric design is intentional.

## Verification checklist before marking verify

- [ ] Unit tests pass: `go test ./cmd/endless-hook/...`
- [ ] All other tests pass: `just test` (Python), `go test ./internal/monitor/...`
- [ ] Manual E2E above produces exactly one "deferring to" log line per worktree-session event and exactly one set of DB rows.
- [ ] Manual E2E from main (no worktree) shows NO "deferring to" log lines and the global runs normally.
- [ ] Loud-warn case verified by deleting `<worktree>/bin/endless-hook` and re-firing: stderr and hook.log both contain the warning, hook still runs, Claude session is not broken.
- [ ] E-1130 plan attached to the task via `endless task update E-1130 --text .endless/plans/E-1130.md` and committed in the same commit.
- [ ] Branch lands on main; worktree removed; `just install` from main; status → `verify`.

fields.prompt:
# Briefing: task lifecycle metadata gap surfaced by E-1144

## Context for you (the delegator)

You spawned session E-1144 ("rename tmux window label format") earlier this work cycle. The session shipped successfully (commit `a25aa58`, merged as `14d1cb6` on main). After it finished, Mike noticed the task in `endless task list` had **no description, no plan, and no outcome** — the only artifact for posterity was the title. Even the most useful piece — that the session diverged from the spawn prompt's assumption that `:` would work as a separator (it doesn't; nor does `.`; underscore was the third try) — was nowhere on the task.

We diagnosed this as a structural gap, not a one-off:

- `task spawn` originally served ad-hoc one-shot delegation. Low metadata bar was fine.
- `task spawn` is now ALSO how you (the delegator running in `now-ready-auto`) launch implementation sessions. Higher cadence, parallel sessions, needs a clean per-task contract.
- The same primitives serve both use cases. There's no enforcement, so the looser bar wins by default.

## What we filed

**E-1175 (decision, status=ready)** — full rationale + decision matrix attached as the task's plan/text. Read this first: `endless task detail E-1175` (then look at the text field). The four-row table at the top is the decision summary.

**E-1176, E-1177, E-1178** — three sibling implementation tasks that bake the decision into the CLI. All cross-linked to E-1175 and to E-1000 (the existing rename `text` → `plan` task).

You'll see them via `endless task list --status ready --status needs_plan` filtered to `relates_to E-1175` once that filter syntax exists, or by following the relations from E-1175.

## Behavioral changes you should adopt NOW (before any of those tasks ship code)

The CLI gates land later. In the meantime, when you file a task you intend to spawn:

1. **Always pass `--description`** to `endless task add`. One sentence: what does this task accomplish, in language a human scanning a list will understand. Do NOT duplicate the prompt's procedural content into description; description is the "what," prompt is the "how."

2. **Simplify the spawn prompt.** Once description carries the "what is this" load, the prompt can drop that content and focus on procedural instructions: where the code lives, how to test, what to commit, what to do when finished. Shorter prompts, cleaner contracts.

3. **End every spawn prompt with an outcome instruction.** Suggested boilerplate:

   > Before flipping status to `verify`, run `endless task update <id> --outcome <one-paragraph summary>`. Cover: final commit ref, what diverged from this prompt's assumptions (if anything), what surprises got filed as follow-up tasks. This is the most valuable artifact of your session — the gap between what was planned and what shipped.

4. **If you file with `--draft`** (because you don't yet know the description), do NOT spawn that task — Mike or you must add the description first. Once E-1178 ships, this becomes a hard error from the CLI; until then it's a discipline.

## What to do with this brief

Acknowledge that you've read and integrated these behavior changes for the rest of this cycle. If anything in the decision (E-1175 plan) reads wrong from your vantage point, flag it now — that decision is `ready` but not `assumed`, so it's still up for revision before code is written against it.

## Reference paths (this filesystem, today)

- Decision: `endless task detail E-1175` — the plan text is the document.
- Implementation tasks: E-1176, E-1177, E-1178 (all `relates_to` E-1175).
- E-1144 (the triggering example): `endless task detail E-1144` — its outcome and text were backfilled at the end of session for the same reason this decision exists.

fields.outcome: Implementation, unit tests, and plan attached. Skip helper added to cmd/endless-hook/claude.go using monitor.FindWorktreeRoot + os.SameFile; loud WARN fallback when worktree binary missing. 7 unit tests pass. End-to-end manual verification still pending (per plan checklist).

payload.title: Distill delegator-session workflow into reusable commands
payload.description: The now-ready-auto orchestrator session currently does worktree creation, prompt authoring, spawn, ff-merge, cleanup, and re-listing by hand on each task. Once we have a few cycles of experience, factor the repeated steps into commands so a delegator session can run for a long time before compaction.

fields.prompt:
You are a worktree-bound Claude Code session spawned by the now-ready-auto orchestrator to implement E-909 end-to-end.

# Your task — E-909

Read the task description: `endless task show E-909`. The "what" lives there, not in this prompt.

# Your environment

- **Worktree**: `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-909` — you started here. Run `pwd` to confirm.
- **Branch**: `task/909-task-deps-llm` — already checked out.
- **Hook override**: this worktree's `.claude/settings.json` points endless-hook at `<worktree>/bin/endless-hook` (already built).
- **Main checkout**: `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless` — DO NOT edit there.

# Procedure

1. `endless task start E-909`.
2. Find the precedent: search `src/endless/cli.py` for an existing `--llm` click option on a sibling command (list/show/next/active/recent/search). Read both the click-option declaration and the function body to understand the LLM output branch.
3. Locate the `task deps` click command in `src/endless/cli.py` (search for `@task_cmd.command("deps")` or similar). Read its current handler — the LLM branch you need to add must mirror what the precedent commands do (compact one-line-per-task format, no decorations).
4. Implement: add the `--llm` option and the LLM-format branch to `task deps`. Match the precedent's option help string style, output style, and field set.
5. Build: `just build` (do NOT run `just install` from this worktree — per repo CLAUDE.md).
6. Manual test from the worktree (use `PYTHONPATH=src endless ...` to run this worktree's code, NOT the globally installed CLI):
   - `PYTHONPATH=src endless task deps E-909` — confirm the human format still works.
   - `PYTHONPATH=src endless task deps E-909 --llm` — confirm compact format. Pick a task with actual deps if E-909 has none; `E-1144`, `E-1135`, or any recently-merged task should have parent/child relations to verify against.
7. Commit on `task/909-task-deps-llm` with a message in the repo style (see `git log --oneline -10`). Co-author footer per CLAUDE.md.
8. **Outcome (REQUIRED before flipping status)**: `endless task update E-909 --outcome "<one paragraph>"`. Cover: final commit ref, what diverged from this prompt's assumptions if anything, what surprises you filed as follow-up tasks. This is the single most valuable artifact of your session — the gap between planned and shipped.
9. `endless task update E-909 --status verify`.
10. Final message to Mike: 1-2 sentence summary plus the tmux switch line below.

# Hard rules

- Stay focused on E-909. New issues become new tasks (`endless task add "..." --description "..." --parent E-909`), never sneak fixes in.
- Do NOT merge to main. Orchestrator handles landing.
- Do NOT run `just install` from this worktree.
- If you create a `.endless/plans/E-909.md` file (or any other plan file), commit it as part of your task commit. Untracked files block worktree cleanup.
- If anything is ambiguous or forces a design choice not covered above — STOP and tell Mike (with the tmux switch line). Do not guess.
- Do NOT mark E-909 `confirmed` or `assumed`. Only `verify`.

# Progenitor — how Mike returns to the orchestrator

You were spawned by the orchestrator session in tmux window `active:endless-now-ready-auto` (window 7 of session `active`). Make this prominent in your final message:

```
tmux select-window -t active:endless-now-ready-auto
```

fields.outcome: Implementation, unit tests (7), and plan attached. Manual end-to-end verified via synthetic payload tests against /usr/local/bin/endless-hook: (1) skip fires when global is invoked from cwd inside a worktree with its own differing bin/endless-hook ('deferring to' logged, exits 0 before activity throttle); (2) no skip when cwd is in main checkout; (3) worktree-binary-missing path logs the WARN with 'just build' hint and falls back to global; (4) when invoked as the worktree binary itself, os.SameFile detects identity and no skip occurs. Verify worktree was created and dropped during verification.

Commit 8bcda5d on task/909-task-deps-llm. Added --llm flag to both 'task deps' and 'task relations' (the latter is the canonical, the former its alias - applied symmetrically). show_relations() in task_cmd.py grew an llm=False parameter; the LLM branch emits '# Relations for E-NNN' followed by one 'rel_display_name E-id status title' line per relation, '(none)' if empty. Format mirrors show_plan's LLM mode (header + plain rows). Verified manually with PYTHONPATH=src against E-496 (blocked_by relation), E-909 (empty), and E-1144 (empty); human format unchanged. No surprises, no follow-ups filed.

payload.title: Add eswt shell function to switch to a task worktree
payload.description: Add 'eswt' (endless switch worktree) shell function to 'endless shell-init', mirroring esu/esp/esf. eswt <task-id> cd's into '.endless/worktrees/e-<id>' for the given task. Used by 'endless task start' output to give users a one-word command instead of a long cd path.

payload.title: Replace absolute home paths with ~ in all CLI output
payload.description: Sweep CLI output across endless and endless-* binaries to replace absolute paths under $HOME with '~/...' in user-facing strings. Absolute paths only acceptable where '~' would literally fail (e.g. paths outside the user's home dir).

payload.title: Auto-inject shell-init into endless-sandbox enter subshell
payload.description: Supervisor in cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go writes a per-shell tempfile (ZDOTDIR/.zshrc for zsh, --rcfile target for bash) that sources the user's normal rc then evals $(endless shell-init), guaranteeing esu/esp/esf are defined in every sandbox subshell with zero user setup. Required so E-1164's worktree-routing logic actually fires when users enter a sandbox. Detects shell from $SHELL basename; falls back to plain interactive shell for unsupported shells (e.g. fish — see maybe-task).

payload.title: Add fish shell support to endless-sandbox enter shell-init injection
payload.description: Extend the per-shell rcfile dispatch in cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go to handle fish (config.fish + XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish path, or fish --init-command). Fish users currently get a working sandbox enter but no esu/esp/esf — a usability gap, not a correctness issue. Defer until a fish user actually asks.

fields.text:
# E-1164 + E-1182 — Worktree-routing shell helpers + auto-inject shell-init

Bundled: E-1164 (routing logic in shell-init) and E-1182 (auto-inject shell-init in `endless-sandbox enter`) ship together because each has limited user-visible value alone. Routing without auto-inject only helps users who manually `eval $(endless shell-init)`. Auto-inject without routing means the wrong endless is called inside a sandbox.

E-1183 (fish support) is filed as `maybe` and out of scope here.

## Problem

Two coupled defects discovered while verifying E-1159:

1. `esu`/`esp`/`esf` call bare `endless`, which resolves to whatever's first on `$PATH` (typically the global `/usr/local/bin/endless`). New subcommands added in a worktree (e.g. `session forget` on `task/1159-…`) are unreachable from helpers until the worktree merges and `just install` runs from main. Surfaced when `esf` errored with `Error: No such command 'forget'.` despite the subcommand existing in the active worktree.

2. Shell helpers are only present if the user has run `eval "$(endless shell-init)"` in their rc. Inside a sandbox subshell launched by `endless-sandbox enter`, this depends on the user having added it themselves. Per Mike (2026-05-04): "every time you require the user to configure something you move farther away from 'it just works'."

## Approach

### Piece A — `_SHELL_INIT_SNIPPET` in `src/endless/cli.py` (E-1164)

Each helper (`esu`/`esp`/`esf`) gains:
- A precondition check: `[ -n "$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID" ]`, with a clear "no active session, run `esu` first" error for `esp`/`esf`. (`esu` is the entry point, so its check is different — it requires a project arg.)
- A worktree-routing check: when `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID` is set, run `endless session cd --target worktree "$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID"` to resolve the worktree path. If non-empty and a directory, set `cmd="uv run --directory $worktree_path endless"`. Otherwise fall back to bare `endless` with no warning (sessions without worktrees are normal).

Snippet also gains `export ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED=1` at the top — proactive marker that other code paths (tests, the auto-inject verification step) can check to know helpers are present in the current shell.

### Piece B — Auto-inject in `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go` (E-1182)

Before launching the subshell:
1. Detect shell from `filepath.Base(shellPath)`.
2. For `zsh`: create tempdir; symlink `~/.zshenv` (if exists) into it; write `<tmp>/.zshrc` containing `[ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ] && source "$HOME/.zshrc"; eval "$(endless shell-init)"`; set `ZDOTDIR=<tmp>` in subshell env; defer `os.RemoveAll(tmp)`.
3. For `bash`: create tempfile containing `[ -f "$HOME/.bashrc" ] && source "$HOME/.bashrc"; eval "$(endless shell-init)"`; pass `--rcfile <tmpfile>` as args; defer cleanup.
4. For other shells (fish etc.): launch as today, no injection. (Filed E-1183.)

`endless` is on PATH (symlinked by `just install`); subshell can resolve `endless shell-init` without a hardcoded path.

The Supervisor.Run() returns after the subshell exits, so cleanup defers fire after the user types `exit`.

### Piece C — Proactive checks (per Mike: "if we can add a check elsewhere we should")

| Where | Check | Action on fail |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Inside auto-inject rcfile (zsh + bash), after the eval | `command -v esu >/dev/null 2>&1` | Print `endless: shell-init failed to load helpers (esu missing)` to stderr. Don't abort. |
| Inside `esp`/`esf` helper bodies | `[ -n "$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID" ]` | Print `<helper>: no active session, run 'esu <project>' first` to stderr; return non-zero. |
| Inside helper routing logic | `[ -d "$worktree_path" ]` after the lookup | Fall back to bare `endless`; no warning (legit case for sessions without worktrees). |
| Inside test fixture | `declare -f esf > /dev/null` after eval'ing shell-init | Test fails with clear diff. |

`functions <name>` (zsh) and `declare -f <name>` / `command -v <name>` (bash, portable) are the right primitives — they query the running shell's symbol table directly, so we never have to guess where the user defined the helper.

## Files modified / added

| File | Change |
| ---- | ------ |
| `src/endless/cli.py` | Rewrite `_SHELL_INIT_SNIPPET`: routing block + precondition checks + `ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED=1` export |
| `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go` | Pre-launch: detect shell, build per-shell rcfile in tempdir, set `ZDOTDIR`/`--rcfile` accordingly, defer cleanup |
| `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter_inject.go` (NEW) | Per-shell rcfile builders: `buildZshInjection`, `buildBashInjection`, dispatch by shell basename |
| `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter_inject_test.go` (NEW) | Unit tests for builders (file contents, ZDOTDIR vs --rcfile dispatch, fish fallback) |
| `tests/test_shell_init.py` (NEW or extend) | Snippet-content tests: `ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED` exported, routing block present, precondition errors present |
| `tests/test_shell_init_routing.py` (NEW) | Subprocess test: launch bash/zsh with the snippet eval'd, assert `declare -f esf` succeeds, assert `esf` prints precondition error when `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID` unset |

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:**
   ```sh
   just test       # python tests pass
   just test-go    # go tests pass (including new enter_inject_test.go)
   ```

2. **Manual — zsh sandbox auto-inject:**
   ```sh
   ./bin/endless-sandbox enter probe-1164
   # In subshell, expect prompt:
   functions esu esp esf | head   # should show all three
   echo $ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED  # should print 1
   esf                              # expect: "esf: no active session, run 'esu <project>' first"
   exit
   ./bin/endless-sandbox destroy probe-1164 --if-exists
   ```

3. **Manual — bash sandbox auto-inject:**
   ```sh
   SHELL=/bin/bash ./bin/endless-sandbox enter probe-1164b
   declare -f esf | head
   exit
   ./bin/endless-sandbox destroy probe-1164b --if-exists
   ```

4. **Manual — routing to worktree:**
   ```sh
   # From a worktree where ENDLESS_SESSION_ID is set:
   esf  # should invoke the worktree's endless via uv run, not the global one
   # Verify by checking that worktree-only subcommands work
   ```

5. **Set both tasks to `verify`:**
   ```sh
   endless task update E-1164 --status verify
   endless task update E-1182 --status verify
   ```

## Out of scope

- E-1183 (fish support) — filed maybe
- Updating `endless register` to nudge users about shell-init in their rc — outside sandboxes, the standard "user adds eval to rc" pattern still applies; can revisit if it becomes friction

# E-1164 + E-1182 — Worktree-routing shell helpers + auto-inject shell-init

Bundled: E-1164 (routing logic in shell-init) and E-1182 (auto-inject shell-init in `endless-sandbox enter`) ship together because each has limited user-visible value alone. Routing without auto-inject only helps users who manually `eval $(endless shell-init)`. Auto-inject without routing means the wrong endless is called inside a sandbox.

E-1183 (fish support) is filed as `maybe` and out of scope here.

## Problem

Two coupled defects discovered while verifying E-1159:

1. `esu`/`esp`/`esf` call bare `endless`, which resolves to whatever's first on `$PATH` (typically the global `/usr/local/bin/endless`). New subcommands added in a worktree (e.g. `session forget` on `task/1159-…`) are unreachable from helpers until the worktree merges and `just install` runs from main. Surfaced when `esf` errored with `Error: No such command 'forget'.` despite the subcommand existing in the active worktree.

2. Shell helpers are only present if the user has run `eval "$(endless shell-init)"` in their rc. Inside a sandbox subshell launched by `endless-sandbox enter`, this depends on the user having added it themselves. Per Mike (2026-05-04): "every time you require the user to configure something you move farther away from 'it just works'."

## Approach

### Piece A — `_SHELL_INIT_SNIPPET` in `src/endless/cli.py` (E-1164)

Each helper (`esu`/`esp`/`esf`) gains:
- A precondition check: `[ -n "$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID" ]`, with a clear "no active session, run `esu` first" error for `esp`/`esf`. (`esu` is the entry point, so its check is different — it requires a project arg.)
- A worktree-routing check: when `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID` is set, run `endless session cd --target worktree "$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID"` to resolve the worktree path. If non-empty and a directory, set `cmd="uv run --directory $worktree_path endless"`. Otherwise fall back to bare `endless` with no warning (sessions without worktrees are normal).

Snippet also gains `export ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED=1` at the top — proactive marker that other code paths (tests, the auto-inject verification step) can check to know helpers are present in the current shell.

### Piece B — Auto-inject in `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go` (E-1182)

Before launching the subshell:
1. Detect shell from `filepath.Base(shellPath)`.
2. For `zsh`: create tempdir; symlink `~/.zshenv` (if exists) into it; write `<tmp>/.zshrc` containing `[ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ] && source "$HOME/.zshrc"; eval "$(endless shell-init)"`; set `ZDOTDIR=<tmp>` in subshell env; defer `os.RemoveAll(tmp)`.
3. For `bash`: create tempfile containing `[ -f "$HOME/.bashrc" ] && source "$HOME/.bashrc"; eval "$(endless shell-init)"`; pass `--rcfile <tmpfile>` as args; defer cleanup.
4. For other shells (fish etc.): launch as today, no injection. (Filed E-1183.)

`endless` is on PATH (symlinked by `just install`); subshell can resolve `endless shell-init` without a hardcoded path.

The Supervisor.Run() returns after the subshell exits, so cleanup defers fire after the user types `exit`.

### Piece C — Proactive checks (per Mike: "if we can add a check elsewhere we should")

| Where | Check | Action on fail |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Inside auto-inject rcfile (zsh + bash), after the eval | `command -v esu >/dev/null 2>&1` | Print `endless: shell-init failed to load helpers (esu missing)` to stderr. Don't abort. |
| Inside `esp`/`esf` helper bodies | `[ -n "$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID" ]` | Print `<helper>: no active session, run 'esu <project>' first` to stderr; return non-zero. |
| Inside helper routing logic | `[ -d "$worktree_path" ]` after the lookup | Fall back to bare `endless`; no warning (legit case for sessions without worktrees). |
| Inside test fixture | `declare -f esf > /dev/null` after eval'ing shell-init | Test fails with clear diff. |

`functions <name>` (zsh) and `declare -f <name>` / `command -v <name>` (bash, portable) are the right primitives — they query the running shell's symbol table directly, so we never have to guess where the user defined the helper.

## Files modified / added

| File | Change |
| ---- | ------ |
| `src/endless/cli.py` | Rewrite `_SHELL_INIT_SNIPPET`: routing block + precondition checks + `ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED=1` export |
| `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go` | Pre-launch: detect shell, build per-shell rcfile in tempdir, set `ZDOTDIR`/`--rcfile` accordingly, defer cleanup |
| `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter_inject.go` (NEW) | Per-shell rcfile builders: `buildZshInjection`, `buildBashInjection`, dispatch by shell basename |
| `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter_inject_test.go` (NEW) | Unit tests for builders (file contents, ZDOTDIR vs --rcfile dispatch, fish fallback) |
| `tests/test_shell_init.py` (NEW or extend) | Snippet-content tests: `ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED` exported, routing block present, precondition errors present |
| `tests/test_shell_init_routing.py` (NEW) | Subprocess test: launch bash/zsh with the snippet eval'd, assert `declare -f esf` succeeds, assert `esf` prints precondition error when `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID` unset |

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:**
   ```sh
   just test       # python tests pass
   just test-go    # go tests pass (including new enter_inject_test.go)
   ```

2. **Manual — zsh sandbox auto-inject:**
   ```sh
   ./bin/endless-sandbox enter probe-1164
   # In subshell, expect prompt:
   functions esu esp esf | head   # should show all three
   echo $ENDLESS_SHELL_INIT_LOADED  # should print 1
   esf                              # expect: "esf: no active session, run 'esu <project>' first"
   exit
   ./bin/endless-sandbox destroy probe-1164 --if-exists
   ```

3. **Manual — bash sandbox auto-inject:**
   ```sh
   SHELL=/bin/bash ./bin/endless-sandbox enter probe-1164b
   declare -f esf | head
   exit
   ./bin/endless-sandbox destroy probe-1164b --if-exists
   ```

4. **Manual — routing to worktree:**
   ```sh
   # From a worktree where ENDLESS_SESSION_ID is set:
   esf  # should invoke the worktree's endless via uv run, not the global one
   # Verify by checking that worktree-only subcommands work
   ```

5. **Set both tasks to `verify`:**
   ```sh
   endless task update E-1164 --status verify
   endless task update E-1182 --status verify
   ```

## Out of scope

- E-1183 (fish support) — filed maybe
- Updating `endless register` to nudge users about shell-init in their rc — outside sandboxes, the standard "user adds eval to rc" pattern still applies; can revisit if it becomes friction

fields.description: Add 'eswt' (endless switch worktree) shell function to 'endless shell-init', mirroring esu/esp/esf. eswt <task-id> cd's into '.endless/worktrees/e-<id>' for the given task. Used by 'endless task start' output to give users a one-word command instead of a long cd path. Must also export ENDLESS_SHELL_HELPERS_LOADED=1 from shell-init so 'task start' can detect whether to print the 'eval "$(endless shell-init)"' bootstrap hint.

fields.outcome: Implementation, 9 unit tests, plan attached. Synthetic-payload E2E verified all 5 cases including the fix for the noisy WARN regression: (A) skip fires for global with override registered + binary present; (B) NO skip and NO warn for worktrees with inherited committed settings.json (the e-1170 noise case — fix confirmed); (C) WARN fires only when override IS registered but binary missing; (D) SameFile detection — no skip when invoked as worktree binary; (E) no activity for main-checkout cwd. Real-world bug fix shipped: gate skip+warn on settings.json content actually referencing the worktree binary.

fields.description: Add 'eswt' (endless switch worktree) shell function to 'endless shell-init', mirroring esu/esp/esf. eswt <task-id> cd's into '.endless/worktrees/e-<id>' for the given task. Used by 'endless task start' output (which uses 'command -v eswt' to bootstrap on demand).

payload.title: Enforce outcome capture before flipping to verify on spawned tasks
payload.description: On the first spawn cycle after E-1175's discipline matrix landed (E-909), the delegate skipped the 'endless task update <id> --outcome' step despite explicit boilerplate in the prompt. Boilerplate-as-instruction is the weakest possible enforcement. Need a stronger gate: either CLI refusal of 'task update --status verify' when outcome is empty on tasks created via spawn, or pre-verify reminder in the hook.

payload.title: Add 'duplicates'/'duplicated_by' relation type to endless task link
payload.description: Current relation types are blocks/implements/replaces/documents/cleans_up/relates_to and inverses. Add 'duplicates'/'duplicated_by' to capture cases where two tasks were filed for the same concern, surfaced by E-986/E-1086 overlap. Without it, duplicates get conflated with 'replaces' (semantically wrong) or 'relates_to' (too weak).

payload.title: Update E-971 plan: lift 'no create verb' lock and align dir convention to e-NNN
payload.description: E-971's plan locked 'no create verb' but the verify-worktree use case (per examples/e-1130-manual-verifiication-recipe.txt) shows the lock didn't account for all real workflows. Lift it with rationale captured. Also align the plan's dir convention ('<id>-<slug>') to the agreed 'e-NNN'/'e-NNN-<differentiator>' used in CLAUDE.md and on disk.

payload.title: Rename worktrees and code paths from task-NNN-<slug> convention to e-NNN
payload.description: A live worktree exists at .endless/worktrees/task-1031-session-focus — a fourth dir convention not matching CLAUDE.md, E-1150's description, or E-971's plan. Audit which code paths produce 'task-NNN-<slug>' and rename to the agreed 'e-NNN'/'e-NNN-<differentiator>' convention. Likely touches E-1150's implementation and the in-progress E-1031 worktree.

payload.title: Design three-tier extension model (project / config / baked-in) including file-format choice
payload.description: Today's extension story is single-tier (.endless/extensions/use.sh from E-1014), project-scoped only. Design a tiered model: baked-in (Endless ships), config (user/machine), project. Also choose declaration format: file-per-hook vs single-config 'scripts' map (package.json-style); the research outcome is captured as a decision feeding the design.

payload.title: Port claude-settings-init from Justfile Python heredoc to Go
payload.description: Today, just claude-settings-init does JSON manipulation via inline Python. Port to Go using a typed struct (no maps; OrderedMap from go-dt/dtx for passthrough keys). Replaces the heredoc; just claude-settings-init becomes a one-liner wrapper. Not a go-cfgstore use case: this generates an artifact for Claude Code, not our own config.

payload.title: Add 'endless worktree add' verb with --purpose, --slug, --base flags
payload.description: Wraps the multi-step verify-worktree creation recipe (git worktree add + companion file + extension hooks) in a single verb. '--purpose verify' produces a sibling 'e-NNN-verify' worktree, auto-detecting the base branch (dev branch if unmerged, else main). Implements the verify workflow the 'no create verb' lock didn't account for.

payload.title: Rename E-1031 'intent' terminology to 'objective'; preserve 'focus' for current attention
payload.description: E-1031 plans 'session-level intent' with a 'primary + children' tree. Rename 'intent'/'primary' to 'objective' ('primary focus' becomes an alias of objective), preserving 'focus' for the dynamic current attention point. Touches E-1031, E-1035, E-1051, E-1055 plans/schema.

payload.title: Build go.work generation as a standalone Go binary or library
payload.description: Today's just go-work-init recipe parses go.mod with grep/sed and writes go.work. Build the same logic in Go using golang.org/x/mod/modfile (cleaner parsing, validation). Pre-positions the logic for any future default Go-stack extension; usable from any worktree-init script regardless of project.

payload.title: Close E-986 as duplicate of E-1086
payload.description: E-986 (post-worktree-create.sh) and E-1086 (extensions/worktree-init.sh) describe the same per-project hook concept with different paths. Keep E-1086 (more recently active, references the existing extensions/use.sh precedent from E-1014). Once E-1185 ships, link via 'duplicated_by' and mark E-986 obsolete.

payload.title: Ship default extensions for specific stacks
payload.description: Once the three-tier extension model lands, Endless can ship baked-in defaults for narrow well-defined stack subsets (not generic 'common stacks'). Initial scope TBD; Go-with-go-pkgs-pattern is the dogfood candidate. Captures the longer-term 'we DO want defaults' intent.

fields.description: How does an endless-managed project handle dependencies being co-developed alongside it, across worktrees, language-agnostic, without per-machine fragility? E-996 is the narrow Go-specific fix (go.work); this task designs the general pattern. See plan for option analysis and cross-cutting concerns. Confirmed 2026-05-05: defaults ARE wanted longer-term (per discussion); not blocking near-term work but should inform option weighting when planning. See E-1194 for the ship-defaults follow-up.

payload.title: Review E-971 worktree ownership model: evaluate alternatives to PID-based lock file
payload.description: E-971's plan specifies an O_EXCL lock file with stored PID and kill -0 staleness check, claimed/released by SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks. Concern raised 2026-05-06: lock files can persist beyond intended lifetime (PID recycling, SessionEnd hook failures during release). Evaluate ephemeral alternatives — process discovery via lsof/proc, flock(2) with sentinel, Endless DB heartbeat, no-tracking — and pick the right tradeoff.

payload.title: Catch deleted-cwd FileNotFoundError and exit gracefully
payload.description: endless task list (and any subcommand reaching Path.cwd()) crashes with a raw FileNotFoundError traceback when the current working directory has been removed (e.g. rm -rf of cwd in another shell). Catch this at the CLI entry point in src/endless/cli.py:main and exit non-zero with a clear message telling the user to cd somewhere else.

fields.text:
# E-1190: Add `endless worktree add` verb

Parent: E-971. Blocked by: E-1186 (the "no create verb" lock must be lifted first to keep the design honest). Relates to: E-1188 (extension model), E-1189 (claude-settings Go port), E-1195 (lock-model review).

## Command surface

```
endless worktree add E-NNN [--purpose PURPOSE] [--ref REF]
```

| Arg | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `E-NNN` | yes | — | Task ID. Must exist. |
| `--purpose` | no | `task` | One of `task`, `verify`. Other values rejected. |
| `--ref` | no | auto | Git ref to branch from. Default: `main` for `task`; auto-detected for `verify`. |

## Naming

| Purpose | Branch | Dir |
|---|---|---|
| `task` | `task/NNN-<auto-slug>` | `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN` |
| `verify` | `task/NNN-verify` | `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN-verify` |

`<auto-slug>` derives from the task title: lowercase, kebab-case, ascii-only, capped at ~30 chars. No `--slug` flag — exposing it adds friction without value; `endless task show E-NNN` is the lookup if needed.

## Auto-detect base ref for `--purpose verify`

When `--ref` is not given:

1. If `task/NNN-<slug>` exists AND is **not** in `git branch --merged main` → use that ref. (Concurrent verify: the variant branch starts from the dev branch's tip and tracks ongoing work.)
2. Otherwise → use `main`. (Post-merge verify: the dev branch is already merged or absent.)

`--ref REF` always overrides. Surfacing the detection result on stderr is helpful but not required.

## Steps performed

1. Validate task `E-NNN` exists.
2. Compute branch + dir names from `--purpose`.
3. Resolve base ref (`--ref` or auto-detect).
4. `git worktree add -b <branch> <dir> <base>`.
5. Write `<dir>/.endless/worktree.json`:
    ```json
    {
      "kind": "task",
      "task_id": "E-NNN",
      "purpose": "task" | "verify",
      "base_branch": "<resolved-base>",
      "branch": "<branch>",
      "created_at": "<time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)>"
    }
    ```
6. Run baked-in: claude-settings generation (Go-ported per E-1189; falls back to `just claude-settings-init` until that lands).
7. Run project extension hook (per E-1188's design — single `worktree-init` for v1; fire-and-forget on failure with a warning to stderr).

Order matters: claude-settings before project hook. If a project hook ever spawns Claude inside the new worktree, the worktree's `.claude/settings.json` should already exist so the spawned session uses the worktree's hook binary.

## `kind` stays `task`

`verify` is a `purpose` (a CLI concern affecting branch/dir naming) on `kind=task` worktrees. The schema enum doesn't grow — verify worktrees are same-task variants, not a new conceptual category.

## Idempotency

If the target dir or branch already exists, exit 1 with a specific message identifying which collided. No silent no-op. No `--force` flag in v1; explicit removal first via `git worktree remove` + `git branch -D`.

## Locking / session adoption

Out of scope for this verb. The verb does not touch `.endless/worktree.lock`. Whether a Claude session that later enters the worktree claims a lock is governed by E-971's SessionStart-hook model — and that model itself is up for review under E-1195.

## Errors

| Condition | Exit | Message |
|---|---|---|
| Task not found | 1 | `Task E-NNN not found` |
| Dir exists | 1 | `Worktree at <path> already exists; remove it first` |
| Branch exists | 1 | `Branch <name> already exists; remove the branch or pick a different --purpose` |
| `--ref` invalid | 1 | (surface git's error verbatim) |
| Not in main checkout | 1 | `Run from main checkout, not a worktree` |
| `--purpose` unrecognized | 1 | `Unknown purpose '<value>'; allowed: task, verify` |

## Verification

Synthetic test plan, run from a scratch checkout:

1. With a fresh test task `E-NNN`, run `endless worktree add E-NNN --purpose verify`. Expect: dir `e-NNN-verify`, branch `task/NNN-verify`, companion file populated, `claude-settings-init` ran, project hook ran (or warned absent).
2. Re-run the same command — expect exit 1 with the dir-exists message.
3. With `--ref feature-x` (where `feature-x` is an existing branch) — expect verify branch starts from `feature-x`'s tip.
4. With a nonexistent task — expect exit 1, task-not-found.
5. From inside an existing worktree — expect exit 1, main-checkout error.
6. With `--purpose task` (default) on a task whose dev worktree was previously dropped — expect dir `e-NNN`, branch `task/NNN-<auto-slug>`, base = `main`.

## Out of scope

- The lock model itself (E-1195).
- An `endless worktree remove` counterpart (separate task if needed).
- Auto-removal of verify worktrees after testing completes.
- Multi-task worktrees.
- A `--force` flag for idempotency override (add only when a real use case emerges).

fields.title: Add 'endless worktree add' verb with --purpose and --ref flags
Update E-971 plan: remove 'no create verb' decision and align dir convention to e-NNN
fields.description: E-971's plan recorded 'no create verb' as a locked decision, derived from a soft preference. The verify-worktree workflow (manual re-testing of completed tasks) doesn't fit either auto-creation trigger and surfaces the gap. Remove the decision, align the dir convention from '<id>-<slug>' to 'e-NNN'/'e-NNN-<differentiator>', capture the rationale as a separate decision entity.
fields.text:
# E-1186: Update E-971 plan — remove "no create verb" lock and align dir convention

Parent / Documents: E-971.

## What's wrong with E-971's current plan

Two items in `.endless/plans/E-971.md` need correction:

1. **"Decisions locked" → "No `create` verb."** Derived from a soft preference ("worktree creation should ideally be a side-effect of session/task start") and transcribed as a hard prohibition. The transcription was a misreading; the original preference left room for cases the auto-triggers don't cover.

2. **Dir convention `.endless/worktrees/<id>-<slug>`.** Does not match what's documented in CLAUDE.md (`e-NNN`), in E-1150's description (`e-NNN`), or what's now on disk (the dirs were renamed to `e-NNN` form during this conversation). E-971's plan is the outlier.

The verify-worktree workflow — creating a fresh worktree to manually re-test a completed/merged task — does not fit either of E-971's auto-creation triggers. It surfaced through dogfooding and required manual `git worktree add` + companion-file edits, proving the gap.

## Edits to `.endless/plans/E-971.md`

### Section "Decisions locked"

**Remove**: "No `create` verb."

**Do not add a softer replacement.** Considered "Worktree creation usually happens as a side-effect; explicit verbs cover the gaps" — rejected as unnecessary. The verbs document themselves in their own sections; an overarching meta-rule is just ceremony.

**Keep**: "No `switch` verb." That one is a real technical constraint (a child process can't change its parent shell's cwd), not a misderived preference.

### Section "Layout inside the repo"

Replace `.endless/worktrees/<id>-<slug>` with:

- `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN` (canonical)
- `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN-<differentiator>` (variants — e.g., `e-1130-verify`)

### Section "Worktree creation triggers"

Add a third subsection after **Per-session worktree** and **Task worktree**:

> **Explicit (`endless worktree add`).** For cases neither auto-trigger covers — primarily verify worktrees for manual re-testing of completed work. See E-1190.

In the existing subsections, clarify branch convention: `task/NNN-<slug>` for canonical task worktrees, `task/NNN-<differentiator>` for variants. Slug remains in the *branch* name; only the *dir* name drops it.

## Decision capture

Create a separate decision entity via `endless task add --type decision`:

- **Title**: Removed "No create verb" lock from E-971; verify use case discovered via dogfooding
- **Body**: The original lock was misderived from a soft preference. The verify-worktree workflow does not fit either of E-971's auto-creation triggers. An explicit `worktree add` verb covers the gap without contradicting the design's intent (most worktree creation still happens as a side-effect; the explicit verb is the escape hatch).
- **Link**: `--documents E-971`

## Out of scope

- Lock-file / ownership model review — see E-1195.
- Auditing other plan files for `<id>-<slug>` references — E-1187 covers code paths.
- Adding a new `kind` enum value for verify — verify is a `purpose` on `kind=task` per E-1190.
- Touching the "No `switch` verb" decision — it stands.

## Verification

After edits land:

1. `grep -n "No \`create\` verb" .endless/plans/E-971.md` → no match.
2. `grep -n "<id>-<slug>" .endless/plans/E-971.md` → no match.
3. `grep -n "endless worktree add" .endless/plans/E-971.md` → at least one match in the triggers section.
4. The new decision entity exists and links to E-971 via `documents`.

payload.title: Rename .endless/events to .endless/db-ledger to signal DB durability
payload.description: The 'events' directory naming caused near-data-loss during E-1170's land: those JSONL files are the Endless DB write-ahead log, but their name reads as 'logs' so they were treated as discardable. 'journal' has the same problem (cf. systemd journalctl). Rename to 'db-ledger' to force the 'authoritative database record' mental model. Migration spans Go writer (cmd/endless-event), Python bridge (event_bridge.py), DB replay/rebuild logic, hooks that scan the path, tests, and a one-time directory rename for existing installs with backward-compat read window.

fields.description: The 'events' directory naming caused near-data-loss during E-1170's land: those JSONL files are the Endless DB write-ahead record (replay-source for DB rebuild), but their name reads as 'logs' so they were treated as discardable. 'journal' has the same problem (cf. systemd journalctl). Rename to 'db-ledger' / 'db-entries-<id>.jsonl' to force the 'authoritative database record' mental model — ledger and entries are canonical accounting terms; losing one is catastrophic in every domain that uses them. Migration spans Go writer (cmd/endless-event), Python bridge (event_bridge.py), DB replay/rebuild logic, hooks that scan the path, tests, and a one-time directory rename for existing installs with backward-compat read window. Open at plan time: keep current host-fp+sequence identifier vs. switch to UUIDs.

fields.text:
# E-1197 — Rename .endless/events/ to .endless/db-ledger/ with db-entries-*.jsonl files

## Why this is being filed

On 2026-05-06, during E-1170's land sequence, Claude (this assistant) used `git stash` to clear unrelated state on main so the worktree branch could rebase. The pop kept the stash silently — git's quiet signal that part of the stash couldn't apply — and Claude declared the operation done without diffing the kept stash against the working tree.

Two files silently regressed:
- `.endless/verbs.json`: 8 verb additions (block, detect, port, distill, auto-inject, close, ship, catch) dropped.
- `.endless/events/events-a7f3-000001.jsonl`: 248 events dropped, including the full plan text for E-863, multiple `task.fields_updated` entries, and `task.status_changed` events.

Mike caught the verbs.json regression and pushed back. Forced verification of events.jsonl revealed the same partial-loss pattern — 595 lines in HEAD/working tree vs 843 in the stash. All 248 events were recovered from `stash@{0}` before any permanent loss. Working tree now at 846 lines (843 stash + 3 post-stash events), all valid JSON, monotonic timestamps, no duplicates.

## Why this is a database problem, not a logging problem

Mike clarified after the recovery: `.endless/events/*.jsonl` is the durable write-ahead record for the Endless SQLite database. The DB is rebuilt by replaying these JSONL entries. Lose entries → divergent DB state on next replay. Reorder entries → corrupt rebuild. Drop a file → lose history that no other system records. The "events" naming biases readers (human and LLM) toward "observable telemetry / discardable logs" — exactly the wrong instinct for a file whose loss corrupts the database.

## Naming choice

| Term | Verdict |
|------|---------|
| events, log, event-log, audit log, changelog | All read as observable side-channel telemetry — the existing trap |
| journal | Same trap (cf. systemd `journalctl`) |
| WAL | Strong DB signal but collides with SQLite's own `<db>-wal` files |
| **ledger / entries** | **No log connotation in any major prior art**; canonical accounting pairing; "losing a ledger entry" is catastrophic in every domain that uses the term |

Decision: directory `db-ledger/`, files `db-entries-<id>.jsonl`. The `db-` file prefix keeps individual files self-identifying when they travel out of context (tarballs, backups, bug reports). "Entries" is the canonical pairing for "ledger" so the names reinforce each other instead of repeating a generic prefix.

## Identifier scheme — preserved per prior decision

Mike confirmed there was already a prior decision (not formally recorded; correcting that retroactively here) to use `<host_fp>-<sequence>` for file identifiers rather than full UUIDs. That decision stands. Files become `db-entries-a7f3-000001.jsonl` (current host-fp + 6-digit sequence preserved). Not re-opening UUIDs vs. fingerprint+seq.

## Implementation scope

- Go writer: `cmd/endless-event/...` — change directory and filename pattern.
- Python bridge: `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — match the new path/pattern.
- DB rebuild / replay logic: any code that scans for `events-*.jsonl` needs to look for `db-entries-*.jsonl`.
- Hooks and monitoring scripts that reference the path.
- Tests (pattern matching, fixture paths).
- Docstrings and any internal docs referencing `.endless/events/`.
- One-time migration on existing installs: `mv .endless/events .endless/db-ledger && rename 's/^events-/db-entries-/' .endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl` (or equivalent in code).
- Backward-compat read window: for one release, readers should accept files under both old and new paths so in-flight events emitted before the migration aren't orphaned. Cutover after one release.

## Verification

- Fresh install: writes go to `.endless/db-ledger/db-entries-<host_fp>-000001.jsonl`.
- Migrated install: existing files renamed in place, no event loss, replay produces identical DB state.
- DB rebuild from `.endless/db-ledger/`: matches the pre-rename rebuild byte-for-byte (modulo timestamps).
- Hooks/monitoring still find the file under the new path.
- A subsequent stash dance (or other risky op) on the renamed directory should *no longer* trigger the "this is just logs" misclassification — naming is the load-bearing safeguard.

## Out of scope

- Switching to UUID identifiers (prior decision: keep host-fp + seq).
- Adding a separate manifest file for replay ordering — current filename ordering is sufficient.
- Retroactively renaming git history of `.endless/events/` files. Old commits keep old paths; live tree uses new paths.

payload.title: Rename .endless/events/ to .endless/db-ledger/ with db-entries-*.jsonl files
payload.description: Rename .endless/events/events-<host>-<seq>.jsonl to .endless/db-ledger/db-entries-<host>-<seq>.jsonl. Reason: 2026-05-06 during E-1170's land, the 'events' name biased Claude into treating these JSONL files as discardable logs, and a stash dance silently dropped 248 entries that act as the SQLite DB write-ahead record (recovered before commit, no permanent loss). 'Ledger'+'entries' carry no log connotation in any major prior art ('journal' collides with systemd journalctl). Identifier scheme (host-fp + 6-digit sequence) preserved per prior decision; not re-litigating UUIDs vs. current scheme. Long-form context in .endless/plans/E-1197.md.

payload.title: Decision: zero direct commits to main from endless or Claude — only merges from worktrees
payload.description: Endless and Claude must never produce direct commits to main. Main only receives changes via merges from worktree branches. End-users may commit to main accidentally before learning endless's approach; tooling should help route their changes into worktrees. For endless and Claude the rule is absolute.

payload.title: Decision: zero dirty state in main's working tree from endless or Claude
payload.description: Even uncommitted changes in main's working tree caused by endless or Claude are forbidden — they create the merge-blocked state for other sessions. User-generated dirty state (downloaded research, ad-hoc edits) is tolerated but endless should provide direction to migrate it into a worktree.

payload.title: Decision: plan storage canonical location depends on task lifecycle stage
payload.description: Plans for tasks that have not started implementation live in the Endless DB only. Plans for in-flight tasks live in their implementation worktree's branch (file form). Plans land in main only via the implementation merge. Pre-implementation plan files must not exist on disk; the DB is the staging area.

payload.title: Forbid plan files at .endless/plans/E-NNN.md in main's working tree
payload.description: Plans belong in worktree branches (or DB until implementation begins), never in main directly. Add a deterministic guard (pre-commit hook on main, check in 'endless task update --text', or both) so writing E-NNN.md to main is rejected. End-users should never have to resolve this manually.

payload.title: Refuse 'endless task start E-NNN' if another live session has the task active
payload.description: Worktree-level lock (E-971, E-1195) prevents two sessions in the same worktree but not two sessions creating two different worktrees for the same task — produces parallel branches that conflict at merge time. Add task-level check at 'endless task start': query active sessions for this task ID; refuse with 'task E-NNN is being worked on by session X'. Independent of worktree-level lock; both layers needed.

payload.title: Make commit and plan-storage rules deterministic via hooks and CLI guards
payload.description: Mechanically enforce: (1) one task ID per commit message (pre-commit regex); (2) no direct commits to main from endless or Claude (pre-receive on main, or refuse via endless wrappers); (3) no dirty state in main's working tree from endless or Claude (watchdog warning at session start); (4) plan files added on main only via merge or single-task plan commit; (5) multi-task branches opt-in via worktree.json task_ids[]. Together these turn the rules from memory-dependent to deterministic.

payload.title: Move plan snapshots into per-task subdirectories
payload.description: Currently .endless/plans/snapshots/ is a flat directory with <timestamp>-<hash>.{json,md} files written by the PostToolUse hook (cmd/endless-hook/claude.go). To find all captures for a given task, you have to read each .json's metadata to filter by task ID. Reorganize to .endless/plans/snapshots/<task-id>/<timestamp>-<hash>.{json,md} so users can ls/grep within one directory to find all snapshots for a task. Snapshots without a task ID go to .endless/plans/snapshots/_orphan/ (or similar fallback). Migration: one-time move of existing flat-layout snapshots into the new tree based on each .json's metadata.

payload.title: Record each .endless/db-ledger/ write in git immediately, not just at land time
payload.description: Today .endless/events/*.jsonl (and after E-1197, .endless/db-ledger/db-entries-*.jsonl) only get committed when 'endless worktree land' runs — they accumulate uncommitted on main between landings. Result: any session that runs endless commands fills the working tree with uncommitted database write-ahead state, which then needs cleanup before any subsequent land. Auto-commit on every event emission (or debounced equivalent — to be decided at plan time) so each ledger write becomes part of git history immediately. Hooks into endless-event's emit path. Debounce/batching strategy is open: every change vs. every N events vs. periodic flush — pick at plan time. Sooner-than-later priority because the current friction blocks landing and risks data loss when developers reach for stash to clear main.

payload.title: Validate plan-file path against task's worktree on 'task update --text'
payload.description: When a task has an active worktree, 'endless task update <id> --text <path>' must reject paths that resolve outside that worktree, with an error pointing to the canonical location. Optional partner: 'endless plan new <task-id>' that writes a stub at the canonical path so agents/humans don't have to compute it. Removes a class of cross-checkout pollution that currently has to be caught by per-Claude memory rules.

fields.description: Currently .endless/plans/snapshots/ is a flat directory with <timestamp>-<hash>.{json,md} files written by the PostToolUse hook (cmd/endless-hook/claude.go). To find all captures for a given task, you have to read each .json's metadata to filter by task ID. Reorganize to .endless/plans/snapshots/<task-id>/<timestamp>-<hash>.{json,md} so users can ls/grep within one directory to find all snapshots for a task. Snapshots without a task ID go to .endless/plans/snapshots/<session-id>/ (every snapshot is written from a session, so session-id is the always-available fallback). Migration: one-time move of existing flat-layout snapshots into the new tree based on each .json's metadata.

Today .endless/events/*.jsonl (and after E-1197, .endless/db-ledger/db-entries-*.jsonl) only get committed when 'endless worktree land' runs — they accumulate uncommitted on main between landings. Result: any session that runs endless commands fills the working tree with uncommitted database write-ahead state, which then needs cleanup before any subsequent land. Auto-commit on every event emission so each ledger write becomes part of git history immediately — premium data integrity, no risk of stash-induced loss. Optimization to evaluate at plan time: for commits that haven't been pushed to origin yet, amend successive auto-commits into a single rolling commit. Bounds the commit count without weakening data integrity (each event still becomes part of git history at write time; the amend just rewrites how recent unpushed history is grouped). Sooner-than-later priority because the current friction blocks landing.

payload.title: Record verbs.json changes immediately on endless verb add
payload.description: Today 'endless verb add <name>' modifies .endless/verbs.json without committing. Result: every verb addition leaves the working tree dirty until either (a) someone manually commits, or (b) the next 'endless worktree land' bundles it into the auto-record commit. Both are bad: (a) creates a coordination tax across sessions, (b) couples verbs.json updates to unrelated worktree-land timing. Auto-commit verbs.json directly inside verb_add (single-file commit, message like 'Endless: register verb <name>'), bypassing the task/worktree association. The verb registry is global config, not session work.

payload.title: Fix endless worktree land failing to remove worktree dir due to untracked .endless/worktree.json
payload.description: On every worktree land, the final 'git worktree remove' step fails with 'fatal: <path> contains modified or untracked files' because the per-worktree companion file .endless/worktree.json is intentionally untracked (committing it would rotate every worktree's contents whenever another worktree is created/destroyed). Land currently surfaces this as a non-fatal error and tells the user to run 'git worktree remove' manually. Better: have land explicitly remove the companion file before 'git worktree remove', or pass --force when only the companion file remains. Either way the user shouldn't see a spurious error every land.

fields.description: When a task has an active worktree, 'endless task update <id> --text <path>' must reject paths that resolve outside that worktree, with an error pointing to the canonical location. Optional partner: 'endless plan new <task-id>' that writes a stub at the canonical path so agents/humans don't have to compute it. Once shipped, retire the per-Claude memory ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mikeschinkel-Projects-endless/memory/feedback_commit_plan_files_you_create.md — it becomes redundant with the product enforcement.

payload.title: Remove MigrateLegacyLedger and dual-path reader fallback from Go events package
payload.description: After the one-time E-1197 migration script (forthcoming) runs successfully on this machine and all stale worktrees are migrated, the Go-side defensive code becomes dead weight: internal/events/migration.go (MigrateLegacyLedger + helpers), the call from internal/events/writer.go::NewWriter, and the legacy-path scan in internal/events/reader.go::ReadAllEvents. Remove all three plus their tests; keep only the LedgerDirName/LedgerFilePrefix/LedgerFileSuffix constants. In the wild (fresh clones) no one ever has a .endless/events/ to migrate from, so this code only existed to handle Mike's local environment, which the migration script will have already settled.

payload.title: Delete merged task branches after merge to main
payload.description: Branch refs are redundant once merged: every commit stays reachable via the merge commit on main, the branch name is preserved in the merge commit message ('Merge branch task/NNN-...'), and 'git branch -d' refuses unmerged branches by default. Recovery is 'git branch <name> <merge-commit>^2' if ever needed. The chaos cost of 20+ stale refs in 'git branch' compounds across 40+ active projects and obscures actually-active work. Policy: after a task/* branch lands on main, delete the local ref. Worktrees are dropped per existing convention; the branch deletion is the parallel step for the ref.

payload.title: Automate merged-branch cleanup in Endless
payload.description: Per decision E-1211, after a task/* or e-* branch lands on main, the local ref should be deleted. Automate this in Endless rather than relying on session memory. Likely surface: extend 'endless worktree land' (or whatever lands a task branch) to delete the local branch after a successful merge, AND/OR a post-merge git hook installed by 'endless worktree' setup. Open question: silent-default-on, or require explicit --delete-branch? Prefer silent-default since the decision is unambiguous; the action mirrors 'git branch -d' safety (refuses unmerged).

payload.title: Design adoption-friendly handling of user-produced dirty state in main
payload.description: Endless and Claude have hard rules (E-1199, E-1200, E-1204) against direct commits or dirty state in main. End users will produce both during normal pre-endless workflows; the tooling must not block them. Detect via existing CLAUDECODE / ENDLESS_SESSION_ID env vars (no new vars). Mechanisms: (1) startup tip when main has any dirty state pointing to 'endless worktree extract' (or chosen verb); (2) 'endless worktree extract' command that lifts uncommitted changes from main into a fresh worktree, leaving main clean; (3) reject pre-commit hooks as a mechanism (not composable, agreed elsewhere). Goal: zero-friction adoption — users get value without changing behavior, and behavioral shifts happen voluntarily as they see the tooling.

fields.description: Today 'endless verb add <name>' modifies .endless/verbs.json without committing. Result: every verb addition leaves the working tree dirty until either (a) someone manually commits, or (b) the next 'endless worktree land' bundles it into the auto-record commit. Both are bad: (a) creates a coordination tax across sessions, (b) couples verbs.json updates to unrelated worktree-land timing. Original proposal was to auto-commit inside verb_add — but per E-1199 (zero direct commits to main from endless or Claude, 2026-05-09), that approach is now forbidden when run from main. Revised approach options: (i) make project-level verbs.json optional and rely on machine-level store ('Added to project + machine' message already shows machine store exists), or (ii) only auto-commit when running from inside a worktree, never main. Resolve before implementation.

fields.prompt:
# Implement E-1186

You are in worktree `.endless/worktrees/e-1186` on branch `task/1186-remove-no-create-verb`. The plan is committed at `.endless/plans/E-1186.md` — read it first.

## Steps

1. **Edit `.endless/plans/E-971.md`** per the plan's "Edits to .endless/plans/E-971.md" section. Three sub-edits: remove the "No `create` verb" line under "Decisions locked", align the dir convention under "Layout inside the repo" to `e-NNN` / `e-NNN-<differentiator>`, add a third subsection under "Worktree creation triggers" for `endless worktree add`.

2. **Create the decision entity** per the plan's "Decision capture" section:
   ```
   endless task add --type decision "Removed 'No create verb' lock from E-971; verify use case discovered via dogfooding" --description "<body from plan>"
   endless task link <decision-id> --to E-971 --type documents
   ```

3. **Run the Verification checks** at the bottom of the plan. All four must pass:
   - `grep -n "No \`create\` verb" .endless/plans/E-971.md` → no match
   - `grep -n "<id>-<slug>" .endless/plans/E-971.md` → no match
   - `grep -n "endless worktree add" .endless/plans/E-971.md` → at least one match
   - The new decision entity exists and links to E-971 via `documents`

4. **Commit on this branch** (one commit, file edit only — the decision entity is a DB op, not a tracked file):
   ```
   git add .endless/plans/E-971.md
   git commit -m "Update E-971 plan: remove 'no create verb' lock and align dir convention (E-1186)"
   ```
   Use the standard Co-Authored-By footer.

5. `endless task update E-1186 --status verify`

6. Return Mike to his window:
   ```
   tmux switch-client -t %93
   ```

## Hard rules (per E-1199, E-1200)

- Stay in this worktree. **Zero commits to main. Zero edits to main's working tree.**
- The plan file (`.endless/plans/E-1186.md`) is canonical — do NOT modify it during implementation. If learnings require updating the plan, commit that update as a SEPARATE commit BEFORE the implementation commit on this branch.
- "No `switch` verb" in E-971's plan stays untouched. Only "No `create` verb" is removed.
- Don't add a softer replacement for the removed lock. The plan explicitly considered and rejected one.

## Out of scope

- Touching code paths or other plan files for `<id>-<slug>` references — E-1187 handles that.
- Implementing E-1190 or anything else.

fields.description: Endless and Claude must never produce direct commits to main for session work, plan files, or code changes. Main receives those changes only via merges from worktree branches. End-users may produce direct commits by accident before learning endless's approach; tooling should help route their changes into worktrees. EXCEPTION: global-config artifacts (e.g., verbs.json, machine+repo-shared metadata where direct commit is the only way to propagate to parallel sessions on the same project) — for these, endless commits directly on the user's behalf in single-file commits clearly labeled as registry/config updates. The rule's spirit is 'no session-work churn on main', not 'never touch main'.

Today 'endless verb add <name>' modifies .endless/verbs.json without committing. Result: every verb addition leaves the working tree dirty until either (a) someone manually commits, or (b) the next 'endless worktree land' bundles it into the auto-record commit. Both are bad: (a) creates a coordination tax across sessions, (b) couples verbs.json updates to unrelated worktree-land timing. Auto-commit verbs.json directly inside verb_add (single-file commit, message like 'Endless: register verb <name>'), bypassing the task/worktree association. The verb registry is global config — repo-tracked so all sessions/users on this project benefit from discovered verbs, and machine-stored so verbs are available outside project context. Both layers required by design. Direct commit-to-main from verb_add is allowed under E-1199's global-config exception.

Endless and Claude have hard rules (E-1199, E-1200, E-1204) against direct commits or dirty session-work state in main. End users will produce both during normal pre-endless workflows; the tooling must not block them. Detect via existing CLAUDECODE / ENDLESS_SESSION_ID env vars (no new vars).
